name: Legion of Honor

facility.primary_name: Palace of the Legion of Honor

facilities.common_name: Palace of the Legion of Honor

photo_credit.author: Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco (FAMSF)

summary_en: The Legion of Honor's record spans construction to operation. A 1924 Pacific Coast Architect feature describes the California Palace of the Legion of Honor's exterior, finished in California Stucco, claiming "a saving of $150,000 over the cost of stone veneer," and notes its Lincoln Park memorial, given to the city by the Spreckels. 1925 Board of Park Commissioners minutes log payments and an organist for its dedication. A 1970-1971 Fine Arts Museums annual report records Ian McKibbin White sworn in as director and a plea to restore the museum's bus service. 2002 trustee minutes place board meetings and exhibitions at the Legion. It is operated by the Fine Arts Museums, whose FY2025 budget line totals about $24M.
